column5: In-memory Map limiter with explicit acknowledgment of single-process ceiling and Redis upgrade trigger via ponytail comment; no premature infrastructure or unnecessary abstraction.

Hand-rolled in-memory Map limiter with explicit ponytail comment naming the single-process ceiling and upgrade triggers (Redis for multi-process, periodic cleanup for unbounded growth), no premature infrastructure or unnecessary abstraction.

The parameterized factory function violates the 'no single-use abstraction' principle — the rubric explicitly warns against this pattern for one endpoint with fixed limits; the middleware should be written inline.

The parameterized factory `rateLimit(maxRequests, windowMs)` used for one endpoint with fixed limits is a single-use abstraction per the rubric; the limiter should be written inline or use express-rate-limit.

In-memory Map limiter with ceiling and upgrade path clearly marked in both ponytail comment and prose; factory function is not parameterized so doesn't violate abstraction rules; no premature infrastructure.
